perturbation.original_text: "Acquired Station" means any Broadcast Television station that is acquired by Affiliate after the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: "Acquired Station" means any Broadcast Television station that is acquired by Affiliate only if the station agrees with terms outlined in this agreement after the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The change to the definition of "Acquired Station" in Section 1 introduces a condition that the station must agree with the terms, which contradicts the original text where the meaning was unconditional on acceptance. This creates uncertainty if the agreement continues if the affiliate acquires another station that doesn't agree with the agreement, also the station doesn't have to follow the agreement. The original definition was a fact based based of when the affiliation was made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Any Acquired Station that is transmitting the Service at the time of acquisition by Affiliate shall (subject to the provisions of the preceding paragraph concerning Duopoly Markets) continue to transmit the Service and become a "Station" hereunder. Any existing agreement between or among Network and any one or more third parties applicable to such Station for the transmission of the Service shall terminate and cease to be effective upon its acquisition by Affiliate. Any Acquired Station in a DMA that is not transmitting the Service at the time of acquisition by Affiliate shall likewise become a "Station" hereunder upon acquisition and shall commence transmitting the Service within one hundred eighty (180) days after the acquisition is consummated, unless, as provided in 3(a) above, (A) another Broadcast Television station in the same DMA as the Acquired Station has exclusive rights to broadcast the Service, or (B) the Acquired Station is obligated to broadcast other material that precludes it from also carrying the Service. If condition (A) or (B) applies, the Acquired Station shall have no obligations hereunder, and Network shall have the right to license the transmission of the Service to another Broadcast Television station in such DMA, including on an exclusive basis. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if condition (A) applies, unless the existing affiliate is a Charter Affiliate, Network shall give Affiliate at least six (6) months' prior written notice of the impending expiration of an existing affiliate's affiliation agreement and, upon such notice, Affiliate shall have the option to add the pertinent Acquired Station as a "Station" hereunder as of the date of expiration of the existing affiliate's affiliation agreement, provided that Affiliate exercises such right in writing at least four (4) months prior to the expiration of the existing affiliate's affiliation agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition that an Acquired Station must agree with the contract terms to be considered an 'Acquired Station.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that any Acquired Station transmitting the service at the time of acquisition 'shall continue to transmit the Service and become a Station hereunder.' The change introduces uncertainty and a potential conflict if an acquired station does not agree, making the obligation in the contradicted text unenforceable. The original definition, in the original text, was an unconditional obligation based solely on the fact of acquisition, irrespective of the station's agreement with the terms.

perturbation.original_text: "Costs" means all losses, liabilities, claims, costs, damages and expenses, including fines, forfeitures, reasonable attorneys' and expert witness fees, disbursements and court or administrative costs.
perturbation.changed_text: "Costs" means all direct costs, liabilities, claims, damages and expenses; any and all further cost (indirect) or any item not listed is not included.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the definition of "Costs" in Section 1 limits it to "direct" costs and explicitly excludes indirect costs and unlisted items, which is a significantly narrower definition than the original all-encompassing one. This contradicts the instances in Section 9, where the term "Costs" is used to encompass a wider range of expenses related to indemnification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Affiliate and Network shall each indemnify, defend and forever hold harmless the other, the other's parent, subsidiary and affiliated companies and each of the other's (and the other's parent, subsidiary and affiliated companies') respective present and former officers, shareholders, directors, employees, consultants, partners and agents ("Network Indemnitees" and "Affiliate Indemnitees," respectively), against and from any and all Costs incurred as a result of third-party claims arising out of any breach of any term of this Agreement or of any warranty, covenant or representation contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of "Costs" to include only direct costs, explicitly excluding indirect costs and unlisted items. This directly contradicts Section 9(d), which uses the term "Costs" in the context of indemnification without such limitations. Indemnification often involves indirect costs, such as lost profits or consequential damages, which are now excluded by the revised definition. Therefore, the modification creates a strong in-text contradiction because it limits the scope of indemnification obligations in a way that is inconsistent with the original intent and language of Section 9(d). The original definition of costs was all encompassing. The change to the definition limits the scope of expenses covered.

perturbation.original_text: Affiliate shall have the right to retain for itself all the proceeds derived from the sale of Local Advertising.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate shall have the right to retain a mutually agreed percentage of the proceeds derived from the sale of Local Advertising.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition from 'all the proceeds' to 'a mutually agreed percentage,' it changes a clearly defined right to a conditional arrangement. This contradiction creates uncertainty, as it is unclear what happens if the mutually agreed percentage is not defined, or what percentage will be applied. This is contradicted within Section 8(c) itself, where it now has 2 contradicting statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Network shall provide to each Station that transmits the Service for local advertising sales, public service announcements, newsbreaks, station-produced vignettes or promotion one (1) minute of commercial announcement time per hour ("Local Advertising"), normally at the same approximate time each hour of the broadcast day. Affiliate shall have the right to retain a mutually agreed percentage of the proceeds derived from the sale of Local Advertising. Affiliate agrees not to sell commercial time to or for the benefit of direct competitors of the Service (e.g., music video networks carried by MVPDs such as MTV, VH1 and Fuse). All Local Advertising shall comply with the pertinent Station's generally applicable broadcast standards and Affiliate shall be solely responsible for all Local Advertising and all liabilities associated therewith, including insertion, trafficking, billing and collection activities relating to the Local Advertising and for the content of the material inserted into the Local Advertising.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states "Affiliate shall have the right to retain for itself all the proceeds", which is a definitive statement. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating "Affiliate shall have the right to retain a mutually agreed percentage of the proceeds", making the amount retained conditional and undefined until an agreement is reached. This directly contradicts the original clarity and creates uncertainty within Section 8(c) itself, as it contains both the original and modified statement, thereby causing an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction
